chapter: Chapter XV - Of Offences Affecting Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals
content: Whoever voluntarily pollutes or vitiates the atmosphere in any place so as to make it harmful to the health of persons living, working, or passing nearby commits an offence.
ai_summary: Making the atmosphere harmful to public health is punishable with fine.

title: Rash or Negligent Driving on a Public Way
chapter: Chapter XV - Of Offences Affecting Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals
content: Whoever drives any vehicle or rides on any public way in a manner so rash or negligent as to endanger human life or likely to cause hurt or injury to any person commits an offence.
essential_ingredients: Driving or riding on a public way
Endangers human life or likely to cause injury
ai_summary: Driving or riding rashly or negligently on a public way and endangering life is punishable.

title: Rash or Negligent Navigation of Vessel
chapter: Chapter XV - Of Offences Affecting Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals
content: Whoever navigates any vessel in a rash or negligent manner so as to endanger human life or likely to cause hurt or injury to any person commits an offence.
essential_ingredients: Danger to human life or risk of injury
ai_summary: Rash or negligent navigation of a vessel endangering life is punishable.

title: Exhibiting False Light, Mark or Buoy
chapter: Chapter XV - Of Offences Affecting Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals
content: Whoever exhibits any false light, mark, or buoy intending or knowing that it is likely to mislead a navigator commits an offence.
essential_ingredients: False light, mark, or buoy exhibited
Intent or knowledge of misleading navigators
ai_summary: Displaying false navigation signals that may mislead navigators is punishable.

title: Conveying Persons by Water in Unsafe Vessel
chapter: Chapter XV - Of Offences Affecting Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals
content: Whoever knowingly or negligently transports a person for hire by water in a vessel that is unsafe or overloaded so as to endanger life commits an offence.
ai_summary: Carrying passengers in an unsafe or overloaded vessel is punishable.

title: Danger or Obstruction in Public Way or Navigation
chapter: Chapter XV - Of Offences Affecting Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals
content: Whoever by any act or omission relating to property in his possession or control causes danger, obstruction, or injury to any person in a public way or public line of navigation commits an offence.
essential_ingredients: Public way or public navigation route
ai_summary: Causing danger or obstruction in a public way or navigation route is punishable with fine.

title: Negligent Conduct with Poisonous Substance
chapter: Chapter XV - Of Offences Affecting Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals
content: Whoever does any act with a poisonous substance in a rash or negligent manner so as to endanger human life or cause injury, or knowingly or negligently fails to take sufficient precautions against probable danger from such substance, commits an offence.
essential_ingredients: Rash or negligent act, or omission of precautions
Danger to human life or risk of injury
ai_summary: Rash or negligent handling of poisonous substances that endangers life is punishable.

title: Negligent Conduct with Fire or Combustible Matter
chapter: Chapter XV - Of Offences Affecting Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals
content: Whoever rashly or negligently handles fire or combustible matter, or fails to take adequate precautions against danger arising from them, thereby endangering human life or causing injury, commits an offence.
essential_ingredients: Rash or negligent act, or omission of precautions
Danger to human life or injury
ai_summary: Negligent handling of fire or combustible materials is punishable.

title: Negligent Conduct with Explosive Substance
chapter: Chapter XV - Of Offences Affecting Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals
content: Whoever rashly or negligently handles an explosive substance, or fails to take sufficient precautions against probable danger from such substance, thereby endangering human life or causing injury, commits an offence.
essential_ingredients: Rash or negligent act, or omission of precautions
Danger to human life or injury
ai_summary: Rash or negligent handling of explosive substances is punishable.

title: Negligent Conduct with Machinery
chapter: Chapter XV - Of Offences Affecting Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals
content: Whoever rashly or negligently uses machinery, or fails to take adequate precautions regarding machinery under his possession or care, thereby endangering human life or causing injury, commits an offence.
essential_ingredients: Rash or negligent act, or omission of precautions
Danger to human life or injury
ai_summary: Negligent handling or maintenance of machinery that endangers life is punishable.

title: Negligent Conduct in Building Construction, Repair or Demolition
chapter: Chapter XV - Of Offences Affecting Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals
content: Whoever, while constructing, repairing, or demolishing a building, knowingly or negligently fails to take sufficient measures to guard against probable danger to human life from the fall of the building or any part thereof, commits an offence.
essential_ingredients: Failure to take adequate safety measures
Danger to human life from collapse or falling parts
ai_summary: Failure to take proper safety measures during construction, repair, or demolition is punishable.

title: Negligent Conduct with Respect to Animal
chapter: Chapter XV - Of Offences Affecting Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals
content: Whoever knowingly or negligently fails to take sufficient precautions with any animal in his possession to guard against probable danger to human life or grievous hurt commits an offence.
ai_summary: Failure to control a dangerous animal resulting in risk to human life is punishable.

title: Punishment for Public Nuisance
chapter: Chapter XV - Of Offences Affecting Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals
content: Whoever commits a public nuisance in any case not otherwise punishable under this Sanhita commits an offence.
ai_summary: Public nuisance not specifically covered elsewhere is punishable with fine.

title: Continuance of Public Nuisance After Injunction
chapter: Chapter XV - Of Offences Affecting Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals
content: Whoever repeats or continues a public nuisance after being lawfully directed by a public servant not to do so commits an offence.
ai_summary: Continuing a public nuisance despite lawful prohibition is punishable.

title: Sale, Distribution or Circulation of Obscene Material
chapter: Chapter XV - Of Offences Affecting Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals
content: Defines obscene material and punishes selling, distributing, exhibiting, producing, importing, exporting, advertising, or dealing in obscene objects, including electronic content.
exceptions: Material justified for public good in the interest of science, literature, art, learning or other objects of general concern
Material kept or used bona fide for religious purposes
Representations on ancient monuments
Representations in temples or religious vehicles
ai_summary: Publishing, distributing, or dealing in obscene material is punishable.

title: Sale of Obscene Objects to Child
chapter: Chapter XV - Of Offences Affecting Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals
content: Whoever sells, distributes, exhibits, circulates, offers, or attempts to provide obscene material to a child commits an offence.
ai_summary: Providing obscene material to children attracts enhanced punishment.

title: Obscene Acts and Songs
chapter: Chapter XV - Of Offences Affecting Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals
content: Whoever performs obscene acts in public places or sings, recites, or utters obscene words or songs near a public place causing annoyance commits an offence.
ai_summary: Public obscene acts or obscene songs causing annoyance are punishable.

title: Keeping Lottery Office and Unauthorized Lottery Activities
chapter: Chapter XV - Of Offences Affecting Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals
content: Punishes keeping an office for unauthorized lotteries and publishing proposals connected with such lotteries.
ai_summary: Operating or promoting unauthorized lotteries is punishable.

title: Injuring or Defiling Place of Worship or Sacred Object
chapter: Chapter XVI - Of Offences Relating to Religion
content: Whoever destroys, damages, or defiles a place of worship or sacred object with intent to insult religion commits an offence.
ai_summary: Damaging a place of worship or sacred object with intent to insult religion is punishable.

title: Deliberate and Malicious Acts Intended to Outrage Religious Feelings
chapter: Chapter XVI - Of Offences Relating to Religion
content: Whoever deliberately and maliciously insults or attempts to insult the religion or religious beliefs of any class of citizens by words, signs, representations, electronic means, or otherwise commits an offence.
ai_summary: Deliberate acts intended to outrage religious feelings are punishable.

title: Disturbing Religious Assembly
chapter: Chapter XVI - Of Offences Relating to Religion
content: Whoever voluntarily causes disturbance to any assembly lawfully engaged in religious worship or religious ceremonies commits an offence.
ai_summary: Disturbing a lawful religious assembly or ceremony is punishable.

title: Trespassing on Places of Worship or Burial Grounds and Insulting Human Corpses
chapter: Chapter XVI - Of Offences Relating to Religion
content: Whoever, with the intention of wounding the feelings of any person, insulting the religion of any person, or knowing that such feelings or religion are likely to be insulted, commits trespass in a place of worship, burial place, place of funeral rites, depository of the dead, offers indignity to a human corpse, or disturbs funeral ceremonies, commits an offence.
ai_summary: Trespassing in sacred places, insulting corpses, or disturbing funeral ceremonies with intent to insult religious feelings is punishable.

title: Uttering Words or Making Gestures with Intent to Wound Religious Feelings
chapter: Chapter XVI - Of Offences Relating to Religion
content: Whoever deliberately intends to wound the religious feelings of any person by words, sounds, gestures, or objects placed within that person's sight commits an offence.
ai_summary: Deliberately using words, sounds, gestures, or objects to wound religious feelings is punishable.

title: Theft
chapter: Chapter XVII - Of Offences Against Property
content: A person commits theft when, intending to take dishonestly any movable property out of another person's possession without consent, he moves that property in order to take it.

special_case.condition: Value of stolen property less than ₹5,000 and first-time offender
special_case.requirement: Return or restoration of stolen property/value

ai_summary: Dishonestly taking movable property without consent constitutes theft.

title: Snatching
chapter: Chapter XVII - Of Offences Against Property
content: Theft becomes snatching when the offender suddenly, quickly, forcibly seizes, grabs, secures, or takes away movable property from a person or from that person's possession.
ai_summary: Forcibly or suddenly taking property from a person or their possession constitutes snatching.

title: Aggravated Forms of Theft
chapter: Chapter XVII - Of Offences Against Property
content: Enhanced punishment applies when theft is committed in specified protected places or against protected property.
aggravated_circumstances: Theft in a building, tent, or vessel used as a human dwelling
Theft in a place used for custody of property
Theft of any means of transport used for passengers or goods
Theft of articles or goods from a means of transport
Theft of an idol or icon from a place of worship
ai_summary: Theft from dwellings, transport systems, places of worship, or government property attracts enhanced punishment.

title: Theft by Clerk or Servant
chapter: Chapter XVII - Of Offences Against Property
content: Whoever, being a clerk, servant, or employed in such capacity, commits theft of property in the possession of his master or employer commits an offence under this section.
ai_summary: A clerk or servant stealing property belonging to or possessed by the employer is punishable with up to 7 years imprisonment and fine.

title: Theft After Preparation for Causing Death, Hurt or Restraint
chapter: Chapter XVII - Of Offences Against Property
content: Whoever commits theft after making preparations to cause death, hurt, wrongful restraint, or fear thereof for committing theft, escaping after theft, or retaining stolen property commits an aggravated form of theft.
ai_summary: Theft committed after preparing to use violence, restraint, or threats attracts rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine.

title: Extortion
chapter: Chapter XVII - Of Offences Against Property
content: A person commits extortion when he intentionally puts another in fear of injury and thereby dishonestly induces that person to deliver property, valuable security, or anything capable of being converted into a valuable security.
ai_summary: Extortion involves obtaining property through fear, with enhanced punishments where threats involve death, grievous hurt, or serious criminal accusations.

title: Robbery
chapter: Chapter XVII - Of Offences Against Property
content: Robbery occurs when theft or extortion is accompanied by actual or threatened violence, instant death, instant hurt, or instant wrongful restraint.
ai_summary: Robbery is theft or extortion accompanied by violence or threats of immediate harm, carrying severe punishment.

title: Dacoity
chapter: Chapter XVII - Of Offences Against Property
content: When five or more persons jointly commit or attempt to commit robbery, or aid its commission, every such person commits dacoity.

punishment: Rigorous Imprisonment not less than 10 Years

ai_summary: Dacoity is robbery committed by five or more persons and includes severe punishments for preparation, participation, gang membership, and murder during dacoity.

title: Robbery or Dacoity with Attempt to Cause Death or Grievous Hurt
chapter: Chapter XVII - Of Offences Against Property
content: If, during robbery or dacoity, the offender uses a deadly weapon, causes grievous hurt, or attempts to cause death or grievous hurt, enhanced punishment applies.
ai_summary: Use of deadly weapons or causing grievous hurt during robbery or dacoity attracts a minimum imprisonment of 7 years.

title: Attempt to Commit Robbery or Dacoity When Armed with Deadly Weapon
chapter: Chapter XVII - Of Offences Against Property
content: Whoever attempts to commit robbery or dacoity while armed with a deadly weapon commits an aggravated offence.
ai_summary: Attempting robbery or dacoity while carrying a deadly weapon attracts a minimum imprisonment of 7 years.

title: Belonging to Gang of Thieves or Robbers
chapter: Chapter XVII - Of Offences Against Property
content: Whoever belongs to any gang of persons associated for habitually committing theft or robbery, other than a gang of dacoits, commits an offence.
ai_summary: Membership in a gang habitually engaged in theft or robbery is punishable.

title: Dishonest Misappropriation of Property
chapter: Chapter XVII - Of Offences Against Property
content: Whoever dishonestly misappropriates or converts any movable property to his own use commits the offence of criminal misappropriation.
ai_summary: Dishonestly converting another person's movable property for personal use constitutes criminal misappropriation.

title: Misappropriation of Property Possessed by Deceased Person
chapter: Chapter XVII - Of Offences Against Property
content: Whoever dishonestly misappropriates property that was in possession of a deceased person and has not yet come into the possession of a person legally entitled to it commits an offence.
ai_summary: Misappropriating property belonging to a deceased person before lawful possession passes to heirs is punishable.

title: Criminal Breach of Trust
chapter: Chapter XVII - Of Offences Against Property
content: A person entrusted with property or dominion over property who dishonestly misappropriates, converts, uses, disposes of, or allows misuse of such property in violation of law or contract commits criminal breach of trust.
ai_summary: Dishonest misuse of entrusted property constitutes criminal breach of trust, with enhanced punishment for fiduciary and public positions.

title: Receiving Stolen Property
chapter: Chapter XVII - Of Offences Against Property
content: Property obtained through theft, extortion, robbery, cheating, criminal misappropriation, or criminal breach of trust is designated as stolen property until it comes into the possession of a person legally entitled to it.
ai_summary: Receiving, retaining, dealing in, or helping conceal stolen property is punishable, with stricter penalties for dacoity-related property.

title: Cheating
chapter: Chapter XVII - Offences Against Property
content: Deceiving a person and dishonestly inducing delivery of property, retention of property, or causing an act or omission resulting in damage, harm, or loss.
ai_summary: Dishonestly deceiving a person to obtain property, benefits, or cause harm.

title: Cheating by Personation
chapter: Chapter XVII - Offences Against Property
content: Cheating by pretending to be another real or imaginary person.
ai_summary: Impersonating another person to deceive someone for gain or advantage.

title: Fraudulent Removal or Concealment of Property to Defeat Creditors
chapter: Chapter XVII - Offences Against Property
content: Removing, concealing, delivering, or transferring property to prevent lawful distribution among creditors.
ai_summary: Fraudulently hiding or transferring assets to avoid creditor claims.

title: Preventing Debt from Being Available for Creditors
chapter: Chapter XVII - Offences Against Property
content: Fraudulently preventing a debt or demand from being available for payment to creditors.
ai_summary: Preventing lawful recovery of debts by creditors through dishonest means.

title: Fraudulent Execution of Deed Containing False Statements
chapter: Chapter XVII - Offences Against Property
content: Executing or participating in a property transfer document containing false statements regarding consideration or beneficiaries.
ai_summary: Using false property transfer documents to deceive others.

title: Fraudulent Concealment or Removal of Property
chapter: Chapter XVII - Offences Against Property
content: Concealing, removing property, or fraudulently releasing valid claims or demands.
ai_summary: Dishonestly hiding property or abandoning claims to defeat lawful rights.

title: Mischief
chapter: Chapter XVII - Offences Against Property
content: Causing destruction, damage, or reduction in value or utility of property with intent to cause wrongful loss.
ai_summary: Intentionally damaging property causing wrongful loss.

title: Mischief by Killing or Injuring Animals
chapter: Chapter XVII - Offences Against Property
content: Killing, poisoning, maiming, or rendering an animal useless.
ai_summary: Causing harm to animals as a form of property damage.

title: Aggravated Forms of Mischief
chapter: Chapter XVII - Offences Against Property
content: Special forms of mischief involving water supply, roads, drainage, navigation, landmarks, fire, or explosives.
ai_summary: Serious property damage affecting public infrastructure, navigation, buildings, or places of worship.

title: Mischief to Rail, Aircraft or Vessel
chapter: Chapter XVII - Offences Against Property
content: Destroying or rendering unsafe railways, aircraft, or large vessels.
ai_summary: Damaging transport systems such as railways, aircraft, or vessels.

title: Running Vessel Aground or Ashore to Commit Theft
chapter: Chapter XVII - Offences Against Property
content: Intentionally running a vessel aground or ashore to commit theft or facilitate dishonest misappropriation of property contained in it.
ai_summary: Deliberately grounding a vessel to steal or misappropriate property.

title: Criminal Trespass and House-Trespass
chapter: Chapter XVII - Offences Against Property
content: Entering or remaining on another person's property with intent to commit an offence, intimidate, insult, or annoy. Entry into dwelling houses, places of worship, or property storage places constitutes house-trespass.
ai_summary: Unauthorized entry onto property with criminal intent.

title: Lurking House-Trespass and House-Breaking
chapter: Chapter XVII - Offences Against Property
content: Defines lurking house-trespass and house-breaking, including six methods of unlawful entry or exit.
ai_summary: Defines concealed house-trespass and house-breaking.

title: Punishment for Lurking House-Trespass or House-Breaking
chapter: Chapter XVII - Offences Against Property
content: Punishments for lurking house-trespass or house-breaking under various aggravating circumstances.
ai_summary: Aggravated punishments for house-breaking and lurking house-trespass.

title: House-Trespass in Order to Commit an Offence
chapter: Chapter XVII - Offences Against Property
content: House-trespass committed to facilitate another offence.
ai_summary: House-trespass committed for carrying out another offence.

title: House-Trespass After Preparation for Hurt or Assault
chapter: Chapter XVII - Offences Against Property
content: House-trespass committed after preparation for causing hurt, assault, wrongful restraint, or fear.
ai_summary: Aggravated house-trespass involving preparation for violence.

title: Dishonestly Breaking Open Receptacle Containing Property
chapter: Chapter XVII - Offences Against Property
content: Breaking open or unfastening a receptacle containing property dishonestly or with intent to commit mischief.
ai_summary: Breaking open containers to access property dishonestly.

title: Making a False Document or False Electronic Record
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
content: Defines making a false document, false electronic record, fraudulent alteration of documents, and causing persons to sign documents through deception, intoxication, or unsoundness of mind.
ai_summary: Foundation definition of forgery involving false documents or electronic records.

title: Forgery
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
content: Making a false document or false electronic record with intent to cause injury, support a claim, obtain property, induce a contract, or commit fraud.
ai_summary: Creating false documents or electronic records with fraudulent intent.

title: Forgery of Court Records, Public Registers and Identity Documents
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
content: Forgery of court records, Aadhaar, voter ID, birth, marriage or burial registers, certificates, powers of attorney and official records.
ai_summary: Forgery involving government records, court records or identity documents.

title: Forgery of Valuable Security, Will or Authority
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
content: Forgery of valuable securities, wills, authorities to adopt, money receipts, property receipts or financial instruments.
ai_summary: Serious forgery involving wills, valuable securities or financial documents.

title: Possession of Forged Documents
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
content: Possessing forged documents while knowing they are forged and intending to use them as genuine.
ai_summary: Knowingly possessing forged documents for fraudulent use.

title: Forged Document and Using Forged Document as Genuine
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
content: Defines forged documents and punishes anyone who knowingly uses forged documents or electronic records as genuine.
ai_summary: Using a forged document is punished the same as creating it.

title: Making or Possessing Counterfeit Seals for Forgery
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
content: Making, possessing or using counterfeit seals, plates or instruments for forgery.
ai_summary: Counterfeit seals and tools used to commit forgery.

title: Counterfeiting Authentication Marks
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
content: Counterfeiting devices or marks used to authenticate documents.
ai_summary: Counterfeiting official authentication marks or security features.

title: Fraudulent Destruction of Will or Valuable Security
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
content: Destroying, cancelling, damaging, concealing or defacing wills, adoption authorities or valuable securities.
ai_summary: Fraudulent destruction or concealment of important legal documents.

title: Falsification of Accounts
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
content: Employees, clerks or servants destroying, altering or falsifying books, records, accounts or electronic records with intent to defraud.
ai_summary: Manipulation of accounts, records or books to commit fraud.

title: Property Mark
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
content: Defines property marks and false property marks used to indicate ownership of goods or movable property.
ai_summary: Using false ownership marks on goods or property.

title: Removing or Altering Property Mark
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
content: Removing, destroying, defacing or adding to a property mark with intent to cause injury.
ai_summary: Tampering with ownership marks on goods or property.

title: Counterfeiting Property Marks
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
content: Counterfeiting private or public property marks and using counterfeit marks as genuine.
ai_summary: Creating or using counterfeit ownership or government marks.

title: Making or Possessing Instruments for Counterfeiting Property Marks
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
content: Making, possessing or keeping any die, plate, instrument or false property mark intended for counterfeiting property marks or falsely indicating ownership of goods.
ai_summary: Possessing or creating tools used to forge property marks.

title: Selling Goods with Counterfeit Property Mark
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
content: Selling, exposing for sale, or possessing for sale goods bearing counterfeit property marks.
ai_summary: Selling goods marked with fake ownership or brand marks.

title: Making False Marks on Goods Receptacles
chapter: Chapter XVIII - Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks
content: Making or using false marks on packages, containers or receptacles to misrepresent the nature, quality or contents of goods.
ai_summary: Using deceptive labels or markings on packages and containers.

title: Criminal Intimidation
chapter: Chapter XIX - Criminal Intimidation, Insult, Annoyance and Defamation
content: Threatening another person with injury to body, reputation or property to cause alarm or compel an act or omission.
ai_summary: Threatening someone to create fear or force action.

title: Intentional Insult with Intent to Provoke Breach of Peace
chapter: Chapter XIX - Criminal Intimidation, Insult, Annoyance and Defamation
content: Intentionally insulting a person knowing it is likely to provoke breach of peace or commission of an offence.
ai_summary: Provocative insults intended to disturb public peace.

title: Statements Conducing to Public Mischief
chapter: Chapter XIX - Criminal Intimidation, Insult, Annoyance and Defamation
content: Publishing false information, rumours or reports causing mutiny, public alarm, communal hatred or public disorder.
ai_summary: Spreading false information that threatens public order or communal harmony.

title: Act Causing Fear of Divine Displeasure
chapter: Chapter XIX - Criminal Intimidation, Insult, Annoyance and Defamation
content: Compelling a person to act or refrain from acting by making them believe they will suffer divine displeasure.
ai_summary: Using religious fear to compel someone's actions.

title: Misconduct in Public by a Drunken Person
chapter: Chapter XIX - Criminal Intimidation, Insult, Annoyance and Defamation
content: Appearing intoxicated in public and causing annoyance to others.
ai_summary: Creating public disturbance while intoxicated.

title: Defamation
chapter: Chapter XIX - Criminal Intimidation, Insult, Annoyance and Defamation
content: Making or publishing imputations that harm another person's reputation, subject to statutory exceptions.
ai_summary: Publishing statements that unlawfully damage a person's reputation.

title: Breach of Contract to Attend on and Supply Wants of Helpless Person
chapter: Chapter XIX - Criminal Intimidation, Insult, Annoyance and Defamation
content: Failure to care for or provide necessities to a helpless person despite being legally bound by contract.
ai_summary: Failure to care for a helpless person despite legal responsibility.

title: Repeal and Savings
chapter: Chapter XX - Repeal and Savings
content: Repeals the Indian Penal Code, 1860 and preserves rights, liabilities, investigations and proceedings already existing under the repealed law.
ai_summary: Repeals IPC and preserves ongoing legal actions under previous law.
